studies.title: Randomized controlled trial to evaluate the effectiveness of a digital health application (Kaia) to prevent back pain and improve quality of life in hospital staff

_verification.reason: The study specifically evaluates the effectiveness of the Kaia app, a digital health application for chronic back pain, in preventing back pain and improving quality of life among hospital staff. The product name 'Kaia' is mentioned multiple times in the trial description, and it is identified as a DiGA approved for chronic back pain.

_classification.reason: The study is explicitly described as a 'randomized controlled trial' which evaluates the effectiveness of a digital health application (Kaia) compared to standard prevention measures. Randomization and control are key features of an RCT.

studies.title: Kaia back pain for the treatment of non-specific low back pain in adult patients - a randomised controlled trial

_verification.reason: The study is specifically about the Kaia Back Pain app, as indicated by the title and detailed description in the trial registration. The manufacturer, Kaia Health Software GmbH, is also mentioned as the developer.

_classification.reason: Keyword match: 3 RCT indicators vs 0 RWE indicators.

studies.title: e-Health and mobile health application for the self-management support of patients with low back pain - a confirmatory trial

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the effectiveness of the 'Kaia' app for low back pain, developed by Kaia Health Software GmbH. The product name 'kaia' and the manufacturer are explicitly mentioned in the trial description and intervention details.

_classification.reason: The study involves random allocation of participants with low back pain to either an intervention group using a medical back app or a control group receiving standard physiotherapy and online information. This randomization to intervention and control groups under controlled conditions defines it as a randomized controlled trial.

studies.title: Innovative Back Pain Therapy with eHealth for our Patients

_verification.reason: The study specifically mentions that patients in the intervention group are treated with a back pain app by Kaia, which aligns with the product Kaia Rückenschmerzen - Rückentraining für Zuhause. The intervention is described as being based on current guidelines, consistent with the product's description.

_classification.reason: The study involves an intervention group receiving a back pain app, suggesting a controlled trial setting. The mention of an 'intervention group' implies random allocation to treatment versus control, characteristic of an RCT.
